_doc: ois interactive-prompt handler table (bug-247). TRANSPORT-NEUTRAL, PURE DATA — consumed by prompt-resolve.sh. Contains ZERO transport verbs (no tmux/capture-pane/send-keys); a transport adapter (ois's tmux wrapper; the mock stdout adapter in test/; a future in-container prompt-supervisor over a different transport) maps the abstract respond tokens to real keystrokes.

_contract.guard: Preconditions that must ALL hold for the handler to be eligible. 'harness' (absent = any) must equal the launching harness; 'launchArgsContains' (absent = unconditional) must be a substring of the joined launch args. FAIL-SAFE: guard miss => fall through, never respond.
_contract.detect: Ordered list of substrings that must ALL appear in the captured pane text for a confident match. A partial/ambiguous pane (any substring missing) => fall through.
_contract.respond: Ordered ABSTRACT key tokens applied on a confident match. A token is either a tmux/terminal key name (Enter, Down, Up, Space, Escape, Tab) or a literal character/string (e.g. "2"). The adapter maps each token to one keystroke.
_contract.once: Fire at most once per session (tracked by handler id).
_contract._rule: A handler responds ONLY when every guard AND every detect substring match. Anything less falls through with no keystroke — cmd_up is the fleet seat-launch/resume path, so ambiguity is fail-closed.

handlers._why: The --dangerously-* dev-channels confirmation banner Claude shows once per session; there is no config bypass, so cmd_up must accept it to reach the hub handshake.

bug-247: `claude -c` resuming a compacted seat shows an Ink select menu (header 'Summarized conversation') with options compact/continue/never. We select option 2 'Resume full session as-is' (value 'continue') to preserve COMPLETE context, not the recommended summary. Default cursor is index 0 (no initialIndex in the binary) so Down moves to index 1, Enter selects. detect is the exact, menu-unique option label. VALIDATION: mechanics are ground-truthed from the claude binary (exact labels + option order + no-initialIndex default) and confirmed live on the IDENTICAL Ink <select> (Down navigates, Enter selects, number-keys ignored); resolver detect/respond are covered by test-prompt-resolve.sh. The end-to-end auto-answer of the LIVE menu is tracked as the node's resume-validate evidence — note the menu is gated by claude's session token/time/auth state (CLAUDE_CODE_RESUME_TOKEN_THRESHOLD etc.), so it surfaces only under those conditions.
